stem: A number n is increased by 4%. If the result is 442, what is the value of n?
rationale: Choice B is correct. Increasing n by 4% gives 1.04n, so 1.04n = 442. Dividing both sides by 1.04 yields n = 442/1.04 = 425. Choice A is incorrect; subtracting 18 (4% of 450) from 442 gives 424, but this is the wrong operation. Choice C is incorrect because 460 would be the value if 442 were 4% less than n in a different sense. Choice D is incorrect because 438 corresponds to subtracting 4 (not 4% of n) from 442.
